analysis.summary: The technician logged into a Cisco network device via PuTTY, configured PuTTY to log all session output to a file named 'Lab-02' on the desktop, and then executed the 'show running-config' command to capture the device's configuration. After successfully capturing the configuration, the technician launched Microsoft Teams and logged in.
actions_taken: Logged into a Cisco network device (172.16.3.3 / 78-R2-LAB-024) via PuTTY
Configured PuTTY session logging to capture 'All session output'
Saved the session log file as 'Lab-02' on the desktop
Executed the 'show running-config' command on the Cisco device
Logged into Microsoft Teams using 'srevanth@technijian.com' account
notable_events: When saving the PuTTY log file, the technician received a prompt indicating a file with the same name ('Lab-02') already existed and chose to overwrite it.

tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician worked methodically, directly navigating to PuTTY logging settings, saving the configuration, and then moving to launch Microsoft Teams. There was a brief, minor interaction with the file save dialog but it was quickly resolved.
